message:
feat(rch): add cargo nextest support for remote test execution

Implements transparent interception and remote execution of cargo nextest
commands:

- Add "nextest" to COMPILATION_KEYWORDS for SIMD keyword detection
- Add CargoNextest variant to CompilationKind enum
- Add classification logic that intercepts "cargo nextest run" and "cargo
  nextest r" (alias) while excluding list/archive/show subcommands
- Add NEVER_INTERCEPT patterns for cargo nextest list/archive/show
- Map CargoNextest to RequiredRuntime::Rust for worker selection
- Map CargoNextest to Rust artifact patterns for transfer

Includes 14 comprehensive tests covering:
- Basic run classification
- Run with flags (--release, --workspace, -p, filters)
- Short alias (r)
- Non-interceptable subcommands (list, archive, show)
- Wrapped commands (time, env vars)
- Serialization and distinction from CargoTest

Closes: remote_compilation_helper-c7ky

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-y9z","title":"E2E Tests: Worker Connectivity and Execution","description":"## Overview
E2E tests for worker connectivity, health checks, and remote execution.

## Test Categories

### 1. Connectivity Tests

#### test_worker_probe_success
```
[e2e::worker] TEST START: test_worker_probe_success
[e2e::worker] CONFIG: worker_id=test-1 host=localhost port=2222
[e2e::worker] PROBE: initiating SSH connection
[e2e::worker] PROBE: ssh_connected latency_ms=45
[e2e::worker] PROBE: running health check command
[e2e::worker] PROBE: health_response="OK rustc 1.75.0, cargo 1.75.0"
[e2e::worker] RESULT: status=healthy slots_available=8
[e2e::worker] TEST PASS: test_worker_probe_success
```

#### test_worker_probe_failure
- Unreachable host → clear error
- Wrong port → connection refused
- Auth failure → permission denied

#### test_worker_probe_timeout
- Slow host → timeout after 10s
- Verify: Timeout doesn't hang test

### 2. Circuit Breaker Tests

#### test_worker_circuit_breaker_opens
```
[e2e::worker] TEST START: test_worker_circuit_breaker_opens
[e2e::worker] CONFIG: worker=bad-worker threshold=3
[e2e::worker] ATTEMPT: 1 result=connection_refused
[e2e::worker] CIRCUIT: failures=1/3 state=CLOSED
[e2e::worker] ATTEMPT: 2 result=connection_refused  
[e2e::worker] CIRCUIT: failures=2/3 state=CLOSED
[e2e::worker] ATTEMPT: 3 result=connection_refused
[e2e::worker] CIRCUIT: failures=3/3 state=OPEN
[e2e::worker] ATTEMPT: 4 result=circuit_open (no connection attempted)
[e2e::worker] VERIFY: state=OPEN rejection_time_ms=0
[e2e::worker] TEST PASS: test_worker_circuit_breaker_opens
```

#### test_worker_circuit_breaker_half_open
- After timeout → HALF_OPEN state
- Single probe attempt allowed
- Success → CLOSED, Failure → OPEN

#### test_worker_circuit_breaker_recovery
- Full cycle: CLOSED → OPEN → HALF_OPEN → CLOSED
- Verify state transitions logged

### 3. Command Execution Tests

#### test_remote_command_success
```
[e2e::worker] TEST START: test_remote_command_success
[e2e::worker] WORKER: test-1
[e2e::worker] COMMAND: echo "hello world"
[e2e::worker] EXECUTE: ssh test-1 'echo "hello world"'
[e2e::worker] STDOUT: hello world
[e2e::worker] STDERR: (empty)
[e2e::worker] EXIT: code=0 duration_ms=123
[e2e::worker] TEST PASS: test_remote_command_success
```

#### test_remote_command_failure
- Command returns non-zero
- Verify: Exit code captured

#### test_remote_command_timeout
- Long-running command
- Verify: Killed after timeout

#### test_remote_command_output_streaming
- Large output (>1MB)
- Verify: Streamed, not buffered

### 4. Worker Selection Tests

#### test_worker_selection_single
- One worker available
- Verify: Selected immediately

#### test_worker_selection_multiple
```
[e2e::worker] TEST START: test_worker_selection_multiple
[e2e::worker] WORKERS: [gpu-1 (slots=8, priority=100), cpu-1 (slots=4, priority=50)]
[e2e::worker] SELECTION: scoring workers...
[e2e::worker] SCORE: gpu-1 = 8 * 100 * 1.0 = 800
[e2e::worker] SCORE: cpu-1 = 4 * 50 * 1.0 = 200
[e2e::worker] SELECTED: gpu-1 (highest score)
[e2e::worker] TEST PASS: test_worker_selection_multiple
```

#### test_worker_selection_all_busy
- No available slots
- Verify: Returns no-worker with reason

#### test_worker_selection_tag_filtering
- Project requires ["rust", "fast"]
- Verify: Only matching workers selected

### 5. SSH Authentication Tests

#### test_ssh_key_auth
- Ed25519, RSA, ECDSA keys
- Verify: All key types work

#### test_ssh_agent_auth
- SSH_AUTH_SOCK set
- Verify: Agent forwarding works

### 6. Edge Cases

#### test_worker_reconnect_after_network_blip
#### test_worker_handles_utf8_output
#### test_worker_handles_binary_output

## Test Summary
| Category | Tests |
|----------|-------|
| Connectivity | 3 |
| Circuit Breaker | 3 |
| Command Execution | 4 |
| Worker Selection | 4 |
| SSH Auth | 2 |
| Edge Cases | 3 |
| **Total** | **19** |

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 19 test cases pass
- [ ] Circuit breaker state transitions logged
- [ ] Selection algorithm explained in logs
- [ ] Timing information captured
- [ ] All edge cases covered","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T09:52:13.842522392-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T12:25:41.703872177-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T12:25:41.703872177-05:00","close_reason":"Completed 21 E2E worker connectivity tests - all passing","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-y9z","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-hxb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T09:54:01.8218326-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-y9z","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-c71","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:32:34.009744623-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ya16","title":"Handle test filtering and special flags (--ignored, --include-ignored, --exact)","description":"## Context & Background

cargo test supports various filtering and flag options that affect execution:

- `cargo test test_name` - Run tests matching pattern
- `cargo test -- --ignored` - Run only ignored tests
- `cargo test -- --include-ignored` - Run all including ignored
- `cargo test -- --exact` - Exact match for test name
- `cargo test -- --skip pattern` - Skip tests matching pattern
- `cargo test -- --show-output` - Show stdout for passing tests

These flags affect:
1. Which tests run (affects duration)
2. Slot requirements (fewer tests = fewer slots needed)
3. Output verbosity

## Current State

Classification handles `cargo test` generically without parsing these flags.
Slot estimation is fixed at 4 regardless of test count.

## Proposed Solution

### 1. Parse test filter for slot estimation
```rust
fn estimate_test_slots(command: &str) -> u32 {
    // Check for specific test names (likely fewer tests)
    if command.contains(\" -- \") {
        // Has test args, might be filtered
        let after_sep = command.split(\" -- \").nth(1).unwrap_or(\"\");
        if !after_sep.starts_with('-') {
            // Has test name filter
            return 2; // Fewer slots for filtered tests
        }
    }
    // Check for --ignored (usually fewer tests)
    if command.contains(\"--ignored\") {
        return 4;
    }
    8 // Default for full test suite
}
```

### 2. Handle --nocapture for output
```rust
// --nocapture means we'll see test stdout
// Ensure streaming handles this properly
if command.contains(\"--nocapture\") || command.contains(\"--show-output\") {
    // Expect more verbose output
}
```

### 3. Consider --test-threads parsing
```rust
fn parse_test_threads(command: &str) -> Option<u32> {
    // cargo test -- --test-threads=4
    let re = regex::Regex::new(r\"--test-threads[=\s](\d+)\").ok()?;
    re.captures(command)
        .and_then(|c| c.get(1))
        .and_then(|m| m.as_str().parse().ok())
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Filtered tests (with name pattern) use fewer slots
- [ ] --test-threads flag respected in slot estimation
- [ ] --nocapture output streams correctly
- [ ] --ignored tests handled without issues

## Files to Modify

- rch/src/hook.rs (slot estimation)
- rch-common/src/patterns.rs (if classification needs update)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T02:13:49.007417317-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T02:13:49.007417317-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-ya16","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-yj8b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T02:17:24.879178843-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-yj4","title":"Implement rch-wkr binary auto-deployment","description":"## Overview
Sub-task of worker provisioning: automatically deploy the rch-wkr binary to remote workers.

## Background
Manual deployment required:
1. scp binary to home dir (can't write directly to /usr/local/bin)
2. ssh to run 'sudo mv ~/rch-wkr /usr/local/bin/'
3. Verify with 'rch-wkr health'

## Requirements
1. Check if rch-wkr is already installed (and version matches)
2. If not, transfer binary via scp/rsync
3. Handle permission elevation (sudo) for /usr/local/bin
4. Fall back to ~/bin or ~/.local/bin if no sudo
5. Verify installation via health check
6. Track deployed version for update management

## Technical Approach
- Check current version: ssh worker 'rch-wkr --version 2>/dev/null || echo "not-installed"'
- Transfer to temp location: scp binary user@host:~/rch-wkr.tmp
- Atomic install: ssh 'sudo mv ~/rch-wkr.tmp /usr/local/bin/rch-wkr && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/rch-wkr'
- Verify: ssh 'rch-wkr health'

## Considerations
- Binary architecture must match worker (x86_64 vs aarch64)
- Cross-compilation for heterogeneous fleets
- Bandwidth optimization: compress during transfer
- Rollback: keep previous version as rch-wkr.bak

## Success Criteria
- rch workers deploy-binary css installs rch-wkr
- Skips if already at correct version
- Works without sudo using fallback paths","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T02:16:32.5201063-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T03:30:37.837958385-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T03:30:37.837958385-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented workers deploy-binary command: finds local binary, checks remote version, deploys via SCP to ~/.local/bin, makes executable, verifies with health check"}
-{"id":"remote_compilation_helper-yj8b","title":"Implement test-aware slot estimation based on RUST_TEST_THREADS","description":"## Context & Background

Currently, slot estimation is hardcoded to 4 cores in hook.rs line 217:
```rust
let estimated_cores = 4;
```

This is suboptimal for tests because:
1. `cargo test` runs test binaries in parallel (default = num CPUs via RUST_TEST_THREADS)
2. Each test binary may also run tests in parallel
3. Tests can be CPU-intensive (especially integration tests)

## Problem

- Build commands compile incrementally, parallelism controlled by -j flag
- Test commands run test binaries in parallel based on RUST_TEST_THREADS
- Using fixed 4 cores underestimates test parallelism needs
- Could lead to worker oversubscription during test runs

## Proposed Solution

1. Add CompilationKind-aware slot estimation in hook.rs:
```rust
fn estimate_cores_for_kind(kind: Option<CompilationKind>, command: &str) -> u32 {
    match kind {
        Some(CompilationKind::CargoTest) => {
            // Check for --test-threads= or RUST_TEST_THREADS
            // Default: request more slots for parallel test execution
            if command.contains(\"--test-threads=1\") {
                2 // Single-threaded tests need less
            } else {
                8 // Default: request more for parallel tests
            }
        }
        Some(CompilationKind::CargoBuild | CompilationKind::CargoCheck) => {
            // Parse -j flag if present, otherwise default
            parse_jobs_flag(command).unwrap_or(4)
        }
        _ => 4 // Default
    }
}
```

2. Consider making this configurable in config.toml:
```toml
[compilation]
default_slots_build = 4
default_slots_test = 8
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Slot estimation varies by CompilationKind
- [ ] cargo test requests more slots than cargo build by default
- [ ] --test-threads flag parsing reduces slot request appropriately
- [ ] -j flag parsing works for build commands
- [ ] Configuration override available

## Files to Modify

- rch/src/hook.rs (estimate_cores_for_kind function)
- rch-common/src/config.rs (add slot estimation config)
- rch-common/src/types.rs (if needed)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T02:11:35.804872712-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T03:44:48.773290761-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"remote_compilation_helper-yj8b","depends_on_id":"remote_compilation_helper-xcvl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-18T02:17:12.684708682-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-ytp","title":"Implement toolchain synchronization across workers","description":"## Overview
Sub-task of worker provisioning: ensure workers have the required Rust toolchains installed and available.

## Background
The dogfooding session revealed workers had different Rust versions:
- css: rustc 1.92.0 (stable)
- csd: rustc 1.94.0-nightly

But the project required nightly-2025-01-01. This caused initial build failure until manually fixed.

## Requirements
1. Detect project toolchain from rust-toolchain.toml
2. Check each worker's installed toolchains
3. Install missing toolchains via rustup
4. Handle case where rustup itself isn't installed
5. Optionally set the required toolchain as default
6. Support toolchain override via environment

## Technical Approach
1. Parse rust-toolchain.toml for channel/version
2. SSH to worker: 'rustup show' to list installed toolchains
3. If missing: 'rustup install <toolchain>'
4. Optionally: 'rustup default <toolchain>'

## Edge Cases
- Worker has no Rust at all (needs rustup installation first)
- Worker behind corporate proxy (rustup download issues)
- Custom toolchain directories
- nightly vs specific date (nightly-2025-01-01)
- Multiple projects with different toolchain requirements

## Dependency on rust-toolchain.toml
The project's rust-toolchain.toml specifies:
```toml
[toolchain]
channel = "nightly-2025-01-01"
```

This must be synced to all workers before compilation can succeed.

## Success Criteria
- rch workers sync-toolchain css installs required toolchain
- rch workers sync-toolchain --all handles entire fleet
- Clear error if rustup not installed (with instructions)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T02:16:33.943622903-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T03:38:10.830534971-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T03:38:10.830534971-05:00","close_reason":"Toolchain synchronization fully implemented and tested: detect_project_toolchain(), sync_toolchain_to_worker(), check_remote_toolchain(), install_remote_toolchain() all working. 64 tests passing. Command 'rch workers sync-toolchain --all' successfully detects required toolchain and syncs to workers."}
 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-zerp","title":"Handle cargo test exit code semantics correctly (101 = test failures vs 1 = build error)","description":"## Context & Background

Cargo test has specific exit code semantics:
- Exit 0: All tests passed
- Exit 1: Build/compilation error (couldn't compile tests)
- Exit 101: Tests ran but some failed

## Current State

Looking at hook.rs lines 294-340, exit code handling is:
```rust
if result.exit_code == 0 {
    // Success - deny local
} else if is_toolchain_failure(&result.stderr, exit_code) {
    // Toolchain issue - allow local fallback
} else {
    // Failed - deny local
}
```

This treats all non-zero as "failed remotely, deny local" which IS correct for tests!

## Verification Needed

1. Confirm exit 101 (test failures) is handled correctly
   - Should: Deny local (agent already saw failures, re-running won't help)
   - Current: Denies - CORRECT

2. Confirm exit 1 (build error) is handled correctly
   - Should: Deny local (same build error would occur locally)
   - Current: Denies - CORRECT

3. Confirm exit 1 with toolchain error allows fallback
   - Should: Allow local (might have toolchain locally)
   - Current: Checks is_toolchain_failure() - CORRECT

## Edge Cases to Consider

### 1. cargo test --no-run
- Only compiles test binaries, doesn't run
- Exit 0 = compiled, Exit 1 = build error
- Should be handled same as cargo build

### 2. Test timeouts
- Individual test timeouts produce test failures
- Overall SSH timeout is different
- Need to distinguish

### 3. Signal exits
- SIGKILL/SIGTERM produce exit codes 128+signal
- May indicate resource issues on worker
- Consider fallback for these?

## Proposed Solution

### 1. Add exit code documentation
```rust
/// Exit codes for cargo test:
/// - 0: All tests passed
/// - 1: Build/compilation error
/// - 101: One or more tests failed
/// - 128+N: Killed by signal N
const CARGO_TEST_ALL_PASSED: i32 = 0;
const CARGO_TEST_BUILD_ERROR: i32 = 1;
const CARGO_TEST_FAILURES: i32 = 101;
```

### 2. Consider signal-killed handling
```rust
fn is_signal_killed(exit_code: i32) -> bool {
    exit_code > 128
}

// In handle_result:
if is_signal_killed(result.exit_code) {
    warn!(\"Remote command killed by signal {}\", result.exit_code - 128);
    // Consider: should we fallback to local?
    // Probably not - signal kill likely means OOM or similar
}
```

### 3. Add more specific exit code logging
```rust
if result.exit_code == 101 {
    info!(\"Tests failed (exit 101), denying local re-execution\");
} else if result.exit_code == 1 {
    info!(\"Build error (exit 1), denying local re-execution\");
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Exit 0 (success) denies local - VERIFY
- [ ] Exit 101 (test failures) denies local - VERIFY
- [ ] Exit 1 (build error) denies local - VERIFY
- [ ] Toolchain failures allow local fallback - VERIFY
- [ ] Signal kills logged appropriately
- [ ] Exit codes documented in code

## Files to Modify

- rch/src/hook.rs (exit code handling documentation and logging)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T02:15:58.687574266-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T02:54:31.668999541-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-18T02:54:31.668999541-05:00","close_reason":"## Already Fully Implemented ✅

All acceptance criteria were already met in the codebase:

### 1. Exit Code Constants (hook.rs:50-58)
```rust
const EXIT_SUCCESS: i32 = 0;
const EXIT_BUILD_ERROR: i32 = 1;
const EXIT_TEST_FAILURES: i32 = 101;
const EXIT_SIGNAL_BASE: i32 = 128;
```

### 2. Specific Exit Code Handling (hook.rs:369-408)
- Signal-killed (128+N): Logs with signal name (SIGKILL, SIGTERM, etc.), denies local
- Test failures (101): "Remote tests failed (exit 101) on {worker}, denying local re-execution"
- Build error (1): "Remote build error (exit 1) on {worker}, denying local re-execution"
- Other non-zero: Generic handling with exit code

### 3. Signal Detection Helpers (hook.rs:631-652)
- `is_signal_killed(exit_code)` - returns Some(signal) if > 128
- `signal_name(signal)` - SIGHUP, SIGINT, SIGKILL, SIGSEGV, SIGTERM, etc.

### 4. Documentation in Code (hook.rs:362-365)
Inline comments explain all exit code semantics

### 5. Tests (hook.rs:1920-1971)
- test_signal_name ✓
- test_exit_code_constants ✓
- test_is_toolchain_failure_basic ✓
- test_exit_code_semantics_documented ✓
- test_process_hook_remote_nonzero_exit_denies ✓

All 38 hook tests pass. No code changes needed."}

@@ -661,6 +661,7 @@ pub(crate) fn required_runtime_for_kind(kind: Option<CompilationKind>) -> Requir
             | CompilationKind::CargoCheck
             | CompilationKind::CargoClippy
             | CompilationKind::CargoDoc
+            | CompilationKind::CargoNextest
             | CompilationKind::Rustc => RequiredRuntime::Rust,
 
             CompilationKind::BunTest | CompilationKind::BunTypecheck => RequiredRuntime::Bun,
@@ -682,7 +683,8 @@ fn get_artifact_patterns(kind: Option<CompilationKind>) -> Vec<String> {
         | Some(CompilationKind::CargoTest)
         | Some(CompilationKind::CargoCheck)
         | Some(CompilationKind::CargoClippy)
-        | Some(CompilationKind::CargoDoc) => default_rust_artifact_patterns(),
+        | Some(CompilationKind::CargoDoc)
+        | Some(CompilationKind::CargoNextest) => default_rust_artifact_patterns(),
         Some(CompilationKind::Gcc)
         | Some(CompilationKind::Gpp)
         | Some(CompilationKind::Clang)
